title: microchilus pumilus
intro: Microchilus pumilus, also known as the dwarf orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which are known to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Microchilus pumilus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and promote wound healing. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and powdering the roots, which can be used in teas, infusions, or as a topical application.
